channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How to watch Constellation Cup 2025: free streams, fixture list for Australia vs New Zealand netball series
item.description: All the ways to watch Constellation Cup live streams online for FREE, with the Diamonds on course to win the title outright for the first time in seven years.
item.dc:creator: Aatif Sulleyman, TechRadar

item.title: The truth about finding cheap airfare, from the head of Google Flights
item.description: Google Flights is one of the most popular flight aggregators on the web. The site lets users search millions of flights to find the best routes and prices that meet their needs. Unsurprisingly, millions of people use Google Flights to find the best deals on holiday tickets.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: If the new Prime Video movie Hedda reminds you of Saltburn, you’re not the only one – but the cast don’t see it
item.description: Is the new movie Hedda really the next Saltburn? The cast ‘don’t see it,’ but you still can stream both on Prime Video.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Valentine, TechRadar

item.title: About 4 in 10 worried about environmental impacts of AI: Survey
item.description: A new poll shows growing concerns among citizens over the potential environmental effects caused by artificial intelligence. The Associated Press-NORC Center poll, released Thursday, found about 4 in 10 U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Conservative activist reaches 'breaking point' with Google
item.description: The Fox News AI Newsletter delivers the latest developments form the world of artificial intelligence, including the technology's challenges and opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why businesses banning AI inevitably lose
item.description: Those AI tools are being trained on our trade secrets. We’ll lose all of our customers if they find out our teams use AI. Our employees will no longer be able to think critically because of the brain rot caused by overreliance on AI. These are not irrational fears.
item.dc:creator: Konstantin Klyagin, Fast Company

item.title: Google TPUs find sweet spot of AI demand, a decade after chip’s debut
item.description: In an AI chip industry that's almost entirely commanded by Nvidia Corp., a Google chip first developed more than 10 years ago especially for artificial intelligence tasks is finally gaining momentum outside its home company as a way to train and run complex AI models.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Why Nvidia just invested in a battery recycling company
item.description: As Nvidia’s value has soared—becoming the first public company to hit $4 trillion in market capitalization earlier this year—it’s been pouring money into AI startups. Its venture arm, NVentures, is also backing less expected bets.
item.dc:creator: Adele Peters, Fast Company

item.title: Trump 'terribly advised' on crypto billionaire pardon: Key supporter
item.description: Venture capitalist Joe Lonsdale, a key supporter of President Trump, suggested Thursday that the president was “terribly advised” on his decision to pardon Binance founder Changpeng Zhao.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: CISA warns Motex Landscope Endpoint Manager has a worrying security flaw, so patch now
item.description: The critical severity bug is being actively exploited in the wild, possibly by ransomware actors, CISA says.
item.dc:creator: Sead Fadilpašić, TechRadar

item.title: In a California farming region, researchers are mapping rural heat to protect farmworkers
item.description: In the summers, the sky is jet black when Raul Cruz arrives at this Imperial Valley sugarcane field to start his day. He chops, cleans and bundles the crop, taking heed as the sun rises. It's hard work, but so is starting at 4 a.m.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and JAE C. HONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: OpenAI seeks to automate ‘computer use’ for Macs in the enterprise
item.description: While AI bots have begun mastering tasks in browsers and on Windows, Mac-using enterprises have largely been overlooked, until now. OpenAI aims to change that with its acquisition of generative AI interface maker Software Applications Incorporated.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI: Latest news and insights
item.description: OpenAI is an artificial intelligence organization comprised of the non-profit OpenAI, Inc. and several for-profit subsidiaries. The company is perhaps best known for its ChatGPT chatbot, which launched in 2022, kicking off a period of massive disruption in the tech industry and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Some of the top Sims 4 creators have just quit the game’s EA Creator Network, citing the ongoing EA sale to Saudi-backed investors – here’s what lilsimsie, James Turner and more have said in their statements
item.description: Here's what some of the top Sims 4 creators have to say on the proposed EA sale deal, including info on who's leaving the EA Creator Network.
item.dc:creator: Jake Green, TechRadar

item.title: The more corporate IT leaders, the greater the need to coexist
item.description: On a global average, CIOs hold 1.8 different positions, according to Foundry’s State of the CIO 2025 survey. So while some see themselves strictly as CIOs, others take on CTO, CISO, chief innovation officer, or COO duties.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Windows 95 icons still exist in Windows 11 today as dusty old relics from another time – here's where to find them
item.description: Nostalgic for Windows 95? Believe it or not, you can still find icons from the dusty old OS kicking about in Windows 11.
item.dc:creator: Darren Allan, TechRadar

item.title: 5 big updates coming to the ChatGPT Atlas browser
item.description: OpenAI is rushing out quality-of-life upgrades for ChatGPT Atlas that promise to make the AI-powered browser better.
item.dc:creator: Eric Hal Schwartz, TechRadar

item.title: Enterprises should not install OpenAI’s new Atlas browser, analysts warn
item.description: Companies that might be eyeing OpenAI’s new ChatGPT Atlas browser should not rush to use it because of potential security risks, analysts said this week. The browser was unveiled on Tuesday after it had been teased for months as a work in progress. It is currently available for MacOS only.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Idaho National Lab testing AI to relieve permitting burdens
item.description: Chris Ritter, the division director for scientific computing and AI at the Idaho National Lab, said using AI to improve basic workflow processes is step one. The post Idaho National Lab testing AI to relieve permitting burdens first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Rivian mobility spinoff readies $4,500 e-bike as first product
item.description: Also, the micromobility startup spun out of Rivian Automotive Inc., has unveiled its first consumer product: an electric bike that will cost up to $4,500.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Nissan adds solar roof to Japan's most popular little EV – and it promises to add 1,800 miles of free range a year
item.description: An extendable solar roof can add useable mileage to Nissan's diminutive EV and the tech is going into production.
item.dc:creator: Leon Poultney, TechRadar

item.title: Musk's rollercoaster year: From boycotts and plunging earnings to a potential trillion-dollar payday
item.description: Elon Musk literally left a government job with a black eye, has seen profits at his car company sink and must worry about federal regulators disrupting his plans to unleash a fleet of driverless taxis on America's roads in the next few years.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Condon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rare dinosaur mummies help scientists recreate their prehistoric lives
item.description: Researchers have unearthed a spooky pair of dinosaur mummies that seem to have been preserved in an unexpected way.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Celtic vs Sturm Graz live stream – How to watch Europa League online, FREE trial, kick-off time
item.description: Celtic take on Sturm Graz: European night sparks, Austrian counter-press chaos, and Kyogo Furuhashi magic — who will roar the loudest in the Europa League 2025?
item.dc:creator: Tom Bailey, TechRadar

item.title: American e-waste is causing a 'hidden tsunami' in Southeast Asia, report says
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Millions of tons of discarded electronics from the United States are being shipped overseas, much of it to developing countries in Southeast Asia unprepared to safely handle hazardous waste, according to a new report released Wednesday by an environmental watchdog.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Serious vulnerability found in Rust library
item.description: Developers creating projects in the Rust programming language, as well as IT leaders with Rust-based applications in their environments, should pay attention to a serious vulnerability found in one of the programming language’s libraries.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI launches Atlas browser to compete with Google Chrome
item.description: OpenAI introduced its own web browser, Atlas, on Tuesday, putting the ChatGPT maker in direct competition with Google as more internet users rely on artificial intelligence to answer their questions.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Meta cutting 600 AI jobs even as it continues to hire more for its superintelligence lab
item.description: MENLO PARK, Calif. (AP) — Meta Platforms is cutting roughly 600 artificial intelligence jobs even as it continues to hire more workers for its superintelligence lab, the company confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio lawmaker proposes comprehensive ban on marrying AI systems and granting legal personhood
item.description: House Bill 469 would prevent AI systems from owning property, serving as executives, or gaining legal personhood in Ohio under Representative Thaddeus Claggett's proposal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google and Apple face extra UK scrutiny over 'strategic' role in mobile platforms
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain’s antitrust watchdog on Wednesday targeted Google and Apple for their “strategic” roles in mobile ecosystems, opening the door for regulators to impose changes to their business practices to improve competition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Intel: Latest news and insights
item.description: More processor coverage on Network World: AMD news and insights | Nvidia news and insights Intel is hoping for a turnaround under its new CEO, Lip-BuTan. Intel’s Q1 2025 revenue was $12.7 billion, flat year-over-year.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Florida test scores rise, attendance improves after cellphone ban
item.description: A new study finds that Florida test scores rose and attendance improved after a school cellphone ban, although short-term disciplinary problems also arose in the state.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: AI PCs
item.description: Virtually every aspect of technology has been affected, and potentially improved, by artificial intelligence (AI). PCs are no exception. While the meaning of “AI PC” is still evolving, it generally refers to a computer specifically designed to excel at AI-powered tasks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Self-propagating worm found in marketplaces for Visual Studio Code extensions
item.description: A month after a self-propagating worm was discovered in the open source NPM code repository, a similar worm has been found targeting Visual Studio Code extensions in open marketplaces.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Big Tech is paying millions to train teachers on AI, in a push to bring chatbots into classrooms
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — On a scorching hot Saturday in San Antonio, dozens of teachers traded a day off for a glimpse of the future. The topic of the day’s workshop: enhancing instruction with artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, Fox 5
